seoTitle: How to Reconcile Accounts on QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to reconcile your bank and credit card accounts in QuickBooks Online so your books consistently match your actual statements.
intro: Reconciling regularly is what keeps your QuickBooks Online records honest against what your bank actually shows. This guide walks through the reconciliation process from start to finish.

steps.heading: Gather your bank statement
steps.body: Have your most recent bank or credit card statement ready with the ending balance and date.

steps.heading: Open the reconcile tool
steps.body: Go to the reconcile section and select the account you're working on.

steps.heading: Enter the statement details
steps.body: Input the ending balance and statement date exactly as shown on your paper or online statement.

steps.heading: Match transactions
steps.body: Check off each transaction that appears on both your statement and in QuickBooks until the difference reaches zero.

faq.question: What does it mean when reconciliation doesn't balance to zero?
faq.answer: A nonzero difference usually means a transaction is missing, duplicated, or entered with the wrong amount, so review your entries against the statement.

faq.question: How often should I reconcile my accounts?
faq.answer: Most businesses reconcile monthly, right after each new bank statement becomes available.

faq.question: Can I reconcile more than one account at a time?
faq.answer: No, reconciliation is done one account at a time to keep the process accurate and manageable.

faq.question: What if I find an error partway through reconciling?
faq.answer: You can pause, fix the transaction, and resume reconciling without losing your progress.
